title: Lepidogrammitis christensenii
intro: Lepidogrammitis christensenii, also known as New Caledonian maidenhair fern, is a species of epiphytic fern native to New Caledonia. This plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Lepidogrammitis christensenii include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, this plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, teas, and topical creams, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and skin irritations.
